ucs.title: [LLM] Tropic Trooper (Pirate Panda) DLL side-loading via signed binary executed from user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts Tropic Trooper's signature initial-foothold tradecraft: dropping a renamed/legitimate signed PE (e.g. vncviewer.exe, vlc.exe, McAfee/Norton stubs, Visual Studio helpers) into a user-writable directory and side-loading a co-located trojanised DLL (Catchamas/USBferry/ChiserClient/TClient loader). The pairing of legit binary + sibling unsigned DLL in %AppData%/%Temp%/%ProgramData% is the dominant Tropic Trooper execution pattern reported by TrendMicro (2018, 2020) and Kaspersky/SecureList (2024 Middle East/Asia campaigns).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as image values(Processes.process_hash) as parent_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("vncviewer.exe","vlc.exe","gup.exe","VsGraphicsDesktopEngine.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","mcods.exe","NisSrv.exe","MsMpEng.exe","python.exe","javaw.exe","OUTLOOK.EXE","chrome.exe") (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_path [| tstats summariesonly=t count values(Filesystem.file_name) as side_dll values(Filesystem.file_hash) as dll_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path | rex field=Filesystem.file_path "(?<process_path>.*)\\\\[^\\\\]+$" | rename Filesystem.dest as dest] | where mvcount(side_dll)>=1 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Tropic Trooper DLL side-loading: signed legit binary outside its install dir loads a co-located DLL
let _trusted_install_dirs = dynamic([@"C:\Program Files\", @"C:\Program Files (x86)\", @"C:\Windows\"]);
let _abusable_loaders = dynamic(["vncviewer.exe","vlc.exe","gup.exe","vsgraphicsdesktopengine.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","mcods.exe","nissrv.exe","msmpeng.exe","python.exe","javaw.exe","outlook.exe","chrome.exe","silverlight.exe","goopdate.exe"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_abusable_loaders)
// loader is running from a user-writable / non-standard directory
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Users\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\Downloads\", @"\PerfLogs\")
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith_cs @"C:\Program Files")
// the DLL is co-located with the loader (classic search-order hijack)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| extend LoaderDir = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath), DllDir = tolower(FolderPath)
| where DllDir == LoaderDir
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
// drop Microsoft-signed system DLLs that happen to live in the same folder
| where not(FolderPath startswith_cs @"C:\Windows\")
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            DllNames = make_set(FileName, 25),
            DllHashes = make_set(SHA256, 25),
            LoadCount = count()
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName,
               InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| where LoadCount >= 1
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel equivalent via Sysmon EID 7 (ImageLoad) — DeviceImageLoadEvents not exposed on Sentinel
let _abusable_loaders = dynamic(["vncviewer.exe","vlc.exe","gup.exe","vsgraphicsdesktopengine.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","mcods.exe","nissrv.exe","msmpeng.exe","python.exe","javaw.exe","outlook.exe","chrome.exe","silverlight.exe","goopdate.exe"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image       = tolower(tostring(EventData.Image)),
         ImageLoaded = tolower(tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded)),
         Signed      = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         User        = tostring(EventData.User),
         ImgHash     = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         LdHash      = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
| extend LoaderName = tostring(split(Image,  "\\")[-1]),
         DllName    = tostring(split(ImageLoaded, "\\")[-1]),
         LoaderDir  = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\"),
         DllDir     = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
| where LoaderName in (_abusable_loaders)
| where LoaderDir == DllDir
| where DllName endswith ".dll"
| where Image has_any ("\\users\\", "\\appdata\\", "\\programdata\\", "\\temp\\", "\\public\\", "\\downloads\\")
| where not(Image startswith "c:\\program files") and not(ImageLoaded startswith "c:\\windows\\")
| where Signed =~ "false" or isempty(Signed)
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            DllList = make_set(DllName, 25), Loads = count()
            by Computer, User, LoaderName, LoaderDir
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Tropic Trooper DLL Side-Load via Legit Binary in User-Writable Path
id: 8a4f1b8e-6f1a-4b51-8a9c-9f3a8a4e9b21
status: experimental
description: Detects a known signed loader binary (Tropic Trooper / Pirate Panda) executing from a user-writable directory and loading a sibling DLL — the canonical KeyBoy/Catchamas/ChiserClient delivery chain.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0081/
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/20/e/tropic-trooper-targets-transportation.html
  - https://securelist.com/new-tropic-trooper-infection-chain-targeting-government-entities/113518/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.001
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0081
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  loader_names:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\vncviewer.exe'
      - '\vlc.exe'
      - '\gup.exe'
      - '\VsGraphicsDesktopEngine.exe'
      - '\mcoemcpy.exe'
      - '\NisSrv.exe'
      - '\python.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\OUTLOOK.EXE'
  loader_in_user_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Users\'
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\Public\'
  dll_unsigned_or_unknown:
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '.dll'
    Signed: 'false'
  filter_system_dll:
    ImageLoaded|startswith: 'C:\Windows\'
  condition: loader_names and loader_in_user_path and dll_unsigned_or_unknown and not filter_system_dll
fields:
  - Image
  - ImageLoaded
  - Signed
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Portable application installers running from %AppData% (e.g. Chocolatey/Scoop). Baseline first.
level: high
ucs.rationale: Side-loading from a user-writable directory using a renamed/portable signed binary is the most consistently reported Tropic Trooper TTP across TrendMicro 2018, 2020, and Kaspersky 2024 reports — they reuse the exact dropper-in-Temp + sibling-DLL pattern across USBferry, Catchamas, ChiserClient, and the 2024 Middle-East government campaign. Generic DLL-load detections fire on every legit installer;

ucs.title: [LLM] Tropic Trooper IIS webshell post-exploit (w3wp.exe spawning recon/cmd) — China Chopper variant
ucs.description: Hunts Tropic Trooper's 2024 IIS-server intrusion pattern (reported by Kaspersky/SecureList against Middle-East and Asian government web hosts): deploying a China-Chopper-derived ASPX webshell on a public-facing IIS server, then driving recon/lateral commands through w3wp.exe. Catches the unmistakable IIS-worker → cmd/powershell/recon child-process tree characteristic of webshell operator activity.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as child_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","wmic.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe","hostname.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval is_iis_app_pool=if(match(user, "(?i)IIS APPPOOL|IUSR|NETWORK SERVICE"),1,0) | where is_iis_app_pool=1 OR like(parent_process,"%w3wp.exe%") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Tropic Trooper IIS webshell operator activity — w3wp.exe spawning interactive/recon binaries
let _recon_bins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","wmic.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe","hostname.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName in~ (_recon_bins)
// IIS service identities — anything else here would mean IIS impersonation, also suspicious
| extend ImpersonatedUser = AccountName
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ImpersonatedUser,
          AppPoolCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildBin    = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
// Optional fan-out enrichment — count recon binaries per host per hour to surface hands-on-keyboard sessions
| summarize Hits          = count(),
            DistinctRecon = dcount(ChildBin),
            ReconBins     = make_set(ChildBin, 25),
            SampleCmds    = make_set(ChildCmd, 25),
            FirstSeen     = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen      = max(Timestamp)
            by DeviceName, ImpersonatedUser, AppPoolCmd, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where DistinctRecon >= 2 or Hits >= 5    // 2+ recon tools or repeated abuse in same hour = operator session
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel equivalent via Windows 4688 process-create — SecurityEvent
let _recon_bins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","wmic.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe","hostname.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in (_recon_bins)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| summarize Hits          = count(),
            DistinctRecon = dcount(NewProcessName),
            ReconBins     = make_set(NewProcessName, 25),
            SampleCmds    = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            FirstSeen     = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen      = max(TimeGenerated)
            by Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where DistinctRecon >= 2 or Hits >= 5
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: IIS Worker Process Spawning Recon/Shell Binary (Tropic Trooper Webshell Operator)
id: 1d2e7c9a-3a1d-4f4e-9e7b-2f1a5b7c8e9d
status: experimental
description: w3wp.exe (IIS application-pool worker) spawning cmd/powershell/recon binaries — high-fidelity webshell post-exploitation, matching Tropic Trooper's 2024 China-Chopper-variant deployments against government IIS servers.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0081/
  - https://securelist.com/new-tropic-trooper-infection-chain-targeting-government-entities/113518/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0081
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_iis:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  child_recon_or_shell:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
  condition: parent_iis and child_recon_or_shell
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Some monitoring/health probes on legacy ASP.NET sites legitimately invoke cmd.exe — baseline per host.
level: high
ucs.rationale: Tropic Trooper's 2024 campaign documented by Kaspersky/SecureList specifically pivoted from custom backdoors to IIS webshells (a China Chopper variant) on government-sector IIS servers in Asia/Middle East — they then drove `whoami / net user / systeminfo / quser / nltest / netstat` recon through the webshell. A w3wp.exe → cmd/recon-binary tree is virtually impossible in normal IIS workloads (web a
